title: Define Entity Interface
text: Define Entity Interface Use this form to define the Database Interface property of and entity, which defines its physical characteristics of in the database. It is only necessary to define your own entity interfaces if you want to change the defaults, such the size, character set, and included entities. For more information, see Database Interface (Entities) . If you have created modeled database interface definitions, click Use modeled to select the one you want to use. This is useful when you want to use the same layout for multiple fields. Properties Name Description Character Set Wide Character Behavior Identification Type Entity Identifier First Occurrence Identifier Next Occurrence Identifier Fixed Length of Entity Fixed Length of Occurrence Comments Related Links Modeled Entities A Modeled Entity is a main development object that represents a logical data aggregation such as a database table,a collection of records, user interface controls, or other data structure. It is an abstract object that is intended for re-use in other objects, such as components, variables, and parameters. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
